title: A Real Pain
logline: Two mismatched cousins travel to Poland on a Holocaust heritage tour to honor their deceased grandmother, confronting family tensions and personal grief along the way.
antagonistOrConflict: The tension between the cousins' different personalities and approaches to grief, family obligation, and processing historical trauma
tone: Bittersweet, contemplative, with moments of dark humor

actBreaks.description: Setup and departure - introduces characters and their dynamic, culminates with meeting the tour group in Warsaw hotel restaurant

validation.ruling: Scene #22 serves as an act break but Scene #24 at the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising Monument (p25, 25%) marks the true end of Act 1, launching the thematic journey into Jewish heritage exploration.

actBreaks.description: The journey deepens - Warsaw exploration, train to Lublin, Majdanek visit, emotional confrontations, ends as they reach the rooftop for their pivotal heart-to-heart

validation.ruling: Scene #84 serves as an act break but Scene #92 in the shoe display barrack (p79, 78%) better represents the end of Act 2, where the Holocaust tour reaches its most visceral moment before the personal resolution phase.

actBreaks.description: Resolution and return - visiting grandmother's hometown, reconciliation, and journey home

validation.ruling: Scene #113 serves as an act break but Scene #116 in the grandmother's alley (p93, 92%) provides the true resolution where Benji shares his meaningful memory about their grandmother caring enough to discipline him, offering emotional closure to their family heritage journey.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: Character-driven journey narrative with geographical and emotional progression
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: Deliberate pacing that mirrors a real trip - slow build in Act 1, intense middle section with Holocaust site visit as emotional center, quiet resolution. Very short scenes (1.4pp average) create episodic feeling within linear structure.

incitingIncident.description: At the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising Monument, James begins the tour by emphasizing Jewish resistance and dispelling myths about passive victimhood.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: EXT. WARSAW GHETTO UPRISING MONUMENT - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #22 is marked as an act break and cannot serve as the inciting incident. Scene #24 at the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising Monument (p25, 25%) is the true inciting incident where James begins the Holocaust tour, setting the central journey in motion and establishing the story's thematic foundation about Jewish resistance and heritage.

midpoint.description: On the hotel roof overlooking Warsaw, the cousins smoke weed and bond. Benji reflects on their past adventures in New York and how David would always fall asleep, revealing both nostalgia and subtle criticism.

validation.ruling: Scene #84 at 76% falls outside the midpoint window (40-60%). Scene #43 on the Warsaw Hotel roof (p39, 39%) serves as the midpoint where the cousins have their first meaningful moment of connection overlooking the city, representing the emotional center before their journey deepens into more challenging territory.

climax.description: Sharing a joint on the roof, David expresses gratitude for their visit while they spot Majdanek concentration camp in the distance. When David asks about Benji's future plans, Benji deflects uncomfortably.

validation.ruling: Scene #105 on the Lublin roof (p84, 83%) is perfectly positioned as the climax. The cousins' stoned conversation while overlooking Majdanek camp represents the emotional peak where they confront their relationship, their family heritage, and their different life paths against the backdrop of historical trauma.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Opening credits roll over a melancholy shot of Benji Kaplan sitting alone at JFK airport, with Chopin's Nocturne playing as the camera slowly zooms in on his absent, melancholic face.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: David hustles through Brooklyn Heights, leaving the first of many voicemails for Benji about their departure time and travel logistics.

scenes.heading: EXT. BQE - DAY
scenes.summary: David's Uber is stuck in traffic on the BQE as he travels to the airport.

scenes.heading: INT. UBER - DAY
scenes.summary: David anxiously leaves multiple voicemails for Benji from the back of his Uber, showing his nervous, over-communicative nature.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: David arrives at JFK and reunites with Benji, who appears with a huge purple backpack. Their warm reunion contrasts David's anxiety with Benji's casual demeanor.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPO RT HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins walk through the airport hallway toward security, with David discretely throwing away yogurt.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT SECURITY — DAY
scenes.summary: At security, David nervously watches as Benji charms the TSA agent Jasmine, displaying his natural charisma while David observes with both anxiety and admiration.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT GATE - DAY
scenes.summary: At the gate, Benji claims he's been 'really great' in recent months, creating an awkward moment where David wants to discuss something serious but can't bring it up.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: On the airplane, Benji gets the window seat and asks David to take the uncomfortable middle seat.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: During the flight, David reveals his digital ad sales job which Benji dismisses as something 'everybody hates,' highlighting their different worldviews and David's insecurity.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the overnight flight, David stays awake watching videos of his son Abe while Benji sleeps peacefully, showing David's homesickness and their different coping mechanisms.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Exhausted at Warsaw airport, both cousins show the strain of travel, with Benji appearing particularly dark and withdrawn.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT CUSTOMS AREA - MORNING
scenes.summary: David nervously watches Benji's bag go through customs while Benji remains completely unfazed by the serious Polish security.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT - MORNING
scenes.summary: After clearing customs, Benji's mood shifts from bleak to suddenly bright when he spots their Heritage Tours driver.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR - MORNING
scenes.summary: In the car to the hotel, David watches videos of his son while Benji stares out the window, both lost in their own thoughts and coping mechanisms.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW - MORNING
scenes.summary: The cousins arrive at the upscale Warsaw Central Hotel.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL - MORNING
scenes.summary: At hotel check-in, Benji receives a mysterious package that has been waiting for days, baffling David who knows nothing about it.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Walking to their hotel room, Benji suddenly becomes affectionate, putting his arm around David and expressing happiness about being together.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In their hotel room, Benji compliments David's feet and reminisces about their grandmother's feet, showing his tendency to notice intimate details and connect them to family memories.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY - MORNING
scenes.summary: The cousins walk down the hotel hallway, with Benji appearing refreshed while David looks sweaty and exhausted.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW LOBBY - MORNIN G
scenes.summary: In the hotel lobby, they meet James, their young British tour guide, who intercepts them and guides them to the restaurant.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: The tour group gathers in the hotel restaurant where James introduces himself as a non-Jewish Oxford scholar of Eastern European studies. Benji immediately charms the group while David observes, and tensions emerge when Benji challenges James's authority about Jewish experience.
scenes.turningPointNote: First major social tension as group dynamics are established and Benji's challenging personality emerges

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: A montage of Warsaw's bustling city life set to Chopin's racing Etude.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW GHETTO UPRISING MONUMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: At the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising Monument, James begins the tour by emphasizing Jewish resistance and dispelling myths about passive victimhood.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks along a street lined with Soviet-era apartment buildings.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji buys water for the group but has no money to pay, requiring David to give him Polish currency, showing their financial dynamic.

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASINSKI GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Walking through Krasinski Garden, Benji philosophizes about how they might have been Polish if not for the war, while David is exhausted and less engaged with these existential thoughts.

scenes.heading: EXT. WALICOW STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks through Warsaw's ruins that abut modern Central European architecture, showing the mix of destruction and rebuilding.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAC GRZYBOWSKI - D AY
scenes.summary: Benji playfully skips along stones jutting from a pond while the patient group waits for him to finish his antics.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAC GRZYBOWSKI CAFE - DAY
scenes.summary: At a cafe, Benji reveals he's learned intimate details about Marcia's recent divorce and relationship troubles, demonstrating his ability to quickly connect with people while David remains more reserved.

scenes.heading: EXT. KREDYTOWA STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks through a bohemian street in Warsaw.

scenes.heading: EXT. Ś WI Ę TOJERSKA STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The group passes a fragment of the old Ghetto Wall.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW UPRISING MONUMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: At the Warsaw Uprising Monument, James explains the tragic complexity of Polish-Russian relations. Benji wants to pose irreverently with the bronze soldiers for photos, creating tension with David who finds this inappropriate, leading to their first real conflict.
scenes.turningPointNote: First direct conflict between the cousins over appropriate behavior and respect for historical tragedy

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group returns to the hotel with good energy from the day, though David feels somewhat left out of the group dynamic.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ELEVATOR — NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the elevator, Benji casually says goodnight to the group members, showing his comfort with social situations.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL HOTEL HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins walk down the hotel hallway toward their room.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benji asks David to join him in the cramped bathroom for privacy.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the bathroom, Benji thanks David intensely for the trip and acknowledges David's social discomfort, while also revealing he brought weed, showing both his gratitude and his assumption that David needs loosening up.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benji tries to open the window to smoke but it only opens a few inches. Despite David's exhaustion, Benji insists they go smoke together, not wanting to waste their first night together in years.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David and Benji ride the elevator up with Benji carrying a joint, both in good spirits for their nighttime adventure.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY OF TOP FLOOR — NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins search for roof access, with David worried about alarms and Benji determined to find a way up, showing their different approaches to risk.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL STAIRWELL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The boys giggle as they race up the stairwell to the roof.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOF — NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the hotel roof overlooking Warsaw, the cousins smoke weed and bond. Benji reflects on their past adventures in New York and how David would always fall asleep, revealing both nostalgia and subtle criticism.
scenes.turningPointNote: Intimate bonding moment that reveals their relationship history and different energy levels

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins ride back down in the elevator in contemplative silence.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In their hotel room, high and eating minibar snacks, the cousins apologize to each other - Benji for calling David a lightweight, David for always falling asleep during their adventures.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David wakes up to find Benji still sleeping surrounded by empty mini vodka bottles.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David FaceTimes with his wife Priya about their son, then showers and takes medication, showing his morning routine and connection to home.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David tries to wake the hungover Benji, who has a pained expression even in sleep, suggesting underlying struggles beneath his cheerful exterior.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW HOTEL HALLWAY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Benji reluctantly follows David through the hotel hallway, clearly struggling with his hangover.

scenes.heading: EXT. WARSAW HOTEL - MORNING
scenes.summary: The group waits outside the hotel as the cousins arrive late. Despite feeling awful, Benji immediately perks up and charms the group, while David apologizes for their tardiness.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW TRAIN STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: The group walks through Warsaw train station, with Benji taking in the sights with childlike wonder

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW TRAIN STATION PLATFORM - DAY
scenes.summary: James leads the group to first class train seats, with David appearing happy while Benji looks skeptical about the arrangements

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: The train moves southeast through the Polish countryside

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji becomes uncomfortable being in first class as Jews on a Polish train, creating tension when he suggests they move cars and disrupts James's tour
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's historical consciousness creates first major friction with the group

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: David searches through crowded train cars looking for Benji after he left the group

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND TRAIN CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: David finds Benji sitting alone and brings him food, as Benji expresses frustration that people want a 'joyride' instead of acknowledging suffering

scenes.heading: EXT. POLAND COUNTRYSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: The train continues speeding through various Polish countryside locations

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: David sleeps while Benji watches him, then David wakes as the train arrives at a station

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASNIK PLATFORM - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins discover they missed their Lublin stop and are stranded in Krasnik, with David angry that Benji didn't wake him

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji devises an elaborate plan to avoid paying train fare on their return journey, spouting anti-capitalist rhetoric while David protests

scenes.heading: INT. FRONT CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins successfully reach first class and celebrate, with Benji now justifying their upgraded seats since they 'earned it'

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN STATION — DAY
scenes.summary: The train arrives at Lublin station and the boys walk along the platform looking for the exit

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins reunite with their irritated tour group, with Benji blaming David for their lateness

scenes.heading: EXT. STAIRS TO CASTLE - DAY
scenes.summary: James leads the group up castle stairs, explaining Lublin's rich history as 'The Jewish Oxford' and emphasizing the day's educational importance

scenes.heading: EXT. BRIDGE FROM ZAMKOWA STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: David discusses religion with Eloge while Marcia connects with Benji about his views on suffering, creating parallel conversations about faith and meaning

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: A montage reveals hidden Jewish history throughout Lublin - former theaters, medical offices, and other sites marked only by small plaques
scenes.turningPointNote: Visual representation of erased Jewish life deepens historical weight

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAVEL STREET - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The group walks along an uneven gravel path at town's edge, with Benji affectionately putting his arm around David

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMETERY – DUSK
scenes.summary: At the Jewish cemetery, James explains the site's 500-year history while Benji grows irritated with James's constant lecturing and approaches to confront him

scenes.heading: EXT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The group arrives at a lively restaurant in old town Lublin for dinner

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: During dinner with kitschy Hava Nagila playing, Benji erupts calling it antisemitic while Marcia shares family stories, leading to Benji performing disruptive music
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's outburst and performance creates major group conflict

scenes.heading: EXT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David leaves the restaurant alone, with Benji's music becoming an intrusive, grating presence in his mind

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ENTRANCE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The distorted version of Benji's music continues as David enters the Lublin hotel lobby

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David's anxiety peaks as he takes medication, fails to reach Priya, and spends a restless night alone examining his bare feet

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David pretends to sleep when Benji returns at 2:40 AM, then watches Benji leave again mysteriously

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: David searches the empty hotel hallway for Benji

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David gets dressed, frustrated and worried about Benji's disappearance

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HALLWAY - MIDDLE OF THE NIGHT
scenes.summary: David continues searching hotel hallways for his missing cousin

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN LOBBY - MIDDLE OF THE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hotel front desk staff haven't seen Benji, leaving David without answers

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - MIDDLE OF THE NIGHT
scenes.summary: David lies in bed angry and worried about Benji's whereabouts

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Benji calls David's room phone claiming everyone is waiting downstairs, revealing he spent the night with Marcia

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ELEVATOR – MORNING
scenes.summary: David rides the hotel elevator down, seething with anger at Benji's behavior

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN LOBBY — MORNING
scenes.summary: David confronts Benji about disappearing, while Benji casually dismisses his cousin's worry and fear
scenes.turningPointNote: David's accumulated frustration with Benji's selfishness reaches breaking point

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - DAY
scenes.summary: The group travels by van toward Majdanek concentration camp as James prepares them for the emotionally taxing visit

scenes.heading: EXT. MAJDANEK - DAY
scenes.summary: The van arrives at the preserved Majdanek concentration camp, an eerily sparse and unadorned Holocaust site
scenes.turningPointNote: Arrival at the concentration camp represents the emotional and thematic climax

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: James explains Majdanek's proximity to Lublin as the group walks toward the entrance, confronting the scope of the preserved camp

scenes.heading: EXT. ENTRANCE AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: James explains how Majdanek was preserved in the Nazis' hasty retreat, as the cousins are overwhelmed by the site's enormity

scenes.heading: INT. BARRACKS - DAY
scenes.summary: The group tours overcrowded barracks designed for 250 but housing up to 1000 prisoners

scenes.heading: INT. UNDRESSING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The group stands silently in the bare undressing room

scenes.heading: INT. SHOWER ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: James reads harrowing personal accounts in the dank, low-ceiling shower room

scenes.heading: INT. GAS CHAMBER - DAY
scenes.summary: In the gas chamber, James points out blue Zyklon B stains on walls while Benji unconsciously reaches for David's shoulder
scenes.turningPointNote: Physical contact between cousins at peak horror shows their fundamental bond

scenes.heading: INT. CREMATORIUM – DAY
scenes.summary: The group silently observes the preserved crematorium ovens, with James offering no commentary

scenes.heading: INT. SHOE DISPLAY BARRACK - DAY
scenes.summary: Benji and David stand together overwhelmed by the mound of victims' discarded shoes

scenes.heading: EXT. MAUSOLEUM - DAY
scenes.summary: The group stands dwarfed beneath the enormous spaceship-like Mausoleum structure

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - DAY
scenes.summary: The shell-shocked group rides back in silence, with Benji openly crying while heartbroken David watches helplessly
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's breakdown and David's empathy begins their reconciliation

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: The emotionally drained group walks toward the hotel entrance

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: James facilitates group processing in the hotel lobby, with members sharing their nausea and trauma before the cousins prepare to leave the tour
scenes.turningPointNote: Formal separation from tour group begins final act

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The cousins walk peacefully through Lublin with a new closeness, moving above a river mural like parallel figures

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTDOOR MARKET - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At an outdoor market, Benji tries on a Polish folk hat, takes a photo, then insists on buying it despite David's practical objections

scenes.heading: EXT. HILL OVERLOOKING CITY - DUSK
scenes.summary: The cousins eat greasy shawarma while hiking up a hill at sunset

scenes.heading: EXT. BREWERY STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Under illuminated flowers and butterflies, Benji playfully jumps to swat decorations while David laughs at his failed attempts

scenes.heading: EXT. LUBLIN STREET OFFICE BUILDING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Benji and David spot a tall building in Lublin and plan to sneak to the roof to smoke Benji's last joint, waiting for a smoker to give them access.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN BUILDING LOBBY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins follow a smoker into the office building lobby, casually nodding at an indifferent security guard as they head to the elevators.

scenes.heading: INT. ELEVATOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In awkward elevator silence, a Polish smoker makes small talk about tomorrow's rain while Benji responds awkwardly, still wearing his Polish hat.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The camera follows the cousins as they emerge from a jiggling rusty door onto the quiet Lublin rooftop overlooking the city.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sharing a joint on the roof, David expresses gratitude for their visit while they spot Majdanek concentration camp in the distance. When David asks about Benji's future plans, Benji deflects uncomfortably.
scenes.turningPointNote: The visual connection to the camp and David's probing about Benji's future creates a pivotal moment of confrontation with both historical trauma and personal uncertainty

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In slow motion, the cousins make their way back to their hotel room in silence after their rooftop encounter.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cousins enter their hotel room silently with a bleak mood. They take turns showering and go to sleep in separate beds without conversation.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL ROOM – MORNING
scenes.summary: The next morning, David and Benji pack their belongings in complete silence, their relationship clearly strained.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY DINING AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins eat breakfast silently in the hotel dining area until David's phone buzzes announcing their taxi's arrival.

scenes.heading: INT. LUBLIN HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Through the lobby window, David and Benji are seen loading their bags into an old, colorful taxi.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRYSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: The taxi travels through Polish countryside, passing a road marker for Krasnystaw, their grandmother's town.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins sit in the back of the taxi en route to their grandmother's town, still maintaining their silence.

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASNYSTAW TOWN SQUARE - DAY
scenes.summary: The taxi arrives at Krasnystaw's quiet town square lined with shops and simple apartment buildings. The cousins exit and retrieve their luggage.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN SQUARE – DAY
scenes.summary: Carrying their luggage across the town square, the cousins search for address number 25 with David consulting his phone for directions.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE STREET NEAR CHURCH - DAY
scenes.summary: David leads Benji around a corner past St. Francis Xavier Church with its massive wooden cross, directing them toward an alley.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY IN FRONT OF HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Finding their grandmother's unremarkable former address in an unchanged alley, Benji shares a memory of being slapped by their grandmother for being late and high to dinner, describing it as the best thing that ever happened to him because it showed she cared more about him than appearances.
scenes.turningPointNote: This revelation about their grandmother's love and Benji's desperate need for meaningful connection provides crucial insight into his character and their family bonds

scenes.heading: EXT. KRASNYTAW TOWN SQUARE - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins walk through the town square with their bags and exit frame as life in the town continues without them.

scenes.heading: I/E. TAXI - NEAR KRASNYSTAW - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins travel in a taxi back toward Warsaw Airport, their pilgrimage to their grandmother's hometown complete.

scenes.heading: I/E. TAXI - WARSAW
scenes.summary: The taxi approaches Warsaw Chopin Airport and turns off at the exit.

scenes.heading: INT. WARSAW AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins walk through Warsaw Airport without speaking but appearing comfortable in each other's presence for the first time in a while.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - DAY
scenes.summary: On the airplane back to America, both cousins are awake and lost in their own thoughts during the flight.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK GATE – DAY
scenes.summary: The cousins exit their flight gate at JFK Airport with other passengers and walk through the terminal.

scenes.heading: INT. JFK BAGGAGE AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: At JFK baggage claim, David invites Benji for dinner with his family, but Benji declines. David then offers to share a cab and drop him at Penn Station, but Benji chooses to stay at the airport, claiming he likes meeting people there.
scenes.turningPointNote: Benji's decision to remain alone at the airport rather than reconnect with family reveals his continued isolation and struggle with belonging

scenes.heading: INT. CAB - DAY
scenes.summary: David rides alone in a cab heading home from the airport, looking out the window at the passing city.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB - DAY
scenes.summary: David's cab passes LeFrak City and the massive Machpelah cemetery, and he contemplates the Jewish history around him - upward mobility and death in an endless cycle - feeling himself part of it all.
scenes.turningPointNote: David's moment of recognition and acceptance of his place in Jewish history and the cycle of life represents his personal growth from the journey

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: David walks down his Brooklyn street toward his apartment, stopping to place a stone from their trip on his building's stoop.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: David enters his apartment, calls out to his family, and embraces his wife Priya and son Abe. He lifts his son in the air and kisses his wife as his life appears truly nice.
scenes.turningPointNote: David's warm reunion with his family demonstrates his successful integration of the journey's lessons into his existing life

scenes.heading: INT. JFK AIRPORT - DAY
scenes.summary: Back at JFK Airport, the camera zooms in on travelers until revealing Benji sitting alone on the same bench where the film opened, watching strangers pass by. The title 'A Real Pain' reappears over his face.
scenes.turningPointNote: The circular ending with Benji alone at the airport, seeking connection with strangers, crystallizes the film's exploration of isolation, pain, and the human need for belonging
